Pollachius pollachius.fieldNote: Mid-water hovering predator with a strongly KINKED lateral line over the pectoral fin, a pronounced under-shot lower jaw, and no chin barbel.

Pollachius virens.fieldNote: Pollack's cousin — dark greenish back, silvery sides, and a near-STRAIGHT pale lateral line (not kinked). Faster, more schooling than pollack; jaw is level rather than under-shot.

Trisopterus luscus.fieldNote: Coppery deep-bodied cod relative with 4-5 dark vertical bands, a single chin barbel, and a dark blotch at the base of each pectoral fin; dense shoals near wrecks and ledges.

Labrus bergylta.fieldNote: Stocky wrasse with thick lips and a long spiny dorsal fin; colours vary from green to red-brown with pale spots.

Labrus mixtus.fieldNote: Two looks: males blaze electric blue with wavy stripes along a yellow-orange body; females and juveniles are pink-orange with three distinctive black saddle blotches along the back near the tail.

Symphodus melops.fieldNote: Small wrasse with a comma-shaped dark eye-spot on the tail base and a curved line behind the eye.

Ctenolabrus rupestris.fieldNote: Slim orange-brown wrasse with TWO diagnostic black spots: one high on the tail base, the other at the front of the dorsal fin. Smallest of the common Welsh wrasses.

Conger conger.fieldNote: Massive grey-brown eel with continuous dorsal-tail-anal fin; usually only the head visible peering from a crevice.

Pholis gunnellus.fieldNote: Slippery ribbon-like fish with a row of dark-and-pale ocelli along the dorsal fin.

Gobius paganellus.fieldNote: Squat little goby with pelvic fins fused into a sucker; pale orange band at the top of the first dorsal.

Pomatoschistus minutus.fieldNote: Pale, near-translucent goby with rows of fine spots; vanishes against sand.

Callionymus lyra.fieldNote: Flat-headed sand-dweller; males flare a tall first dorsal and lyre-shaped tail in display.

Callionymus maculatus.fieldNote: A small sand-dwelling dragonet, cryptically spotted and reticulated in cream and brown. Smaller than the common dragonet; the male's first dorsal fin is raised but not as tall or filamentous as C. lyra. Found on sandy-muddy bottoms from the shallows down to 400m.

Pleuronectes platessa.fieldNote: Right-eyed flatfish with bright orange spots scattered across a brown back.

Limanda limanda.fieldNote: Right-eyed flatfish on sandy bottoms, sandpapery rough scales (the only common UK flatfish with this texture) and a distinctly concave arch in the lateral line above the pectoral fin. No orange spots — plainer than plaice.

Platichthys flesus.fieldNote: A dull brown flatfish of estuaries and tidal rivers, tolerating fresh water — unusual for a flatfish. Rough bony tubercles run along the bases of the dorsal and anal fins and along the lateral line; any orange spots are pale and scattered, unlike the vivid orange of plaice.

Scyliorhinus canicula.fieldNote: Slim sandy-coloured shark covered in small dark spots all over body and fins; two dorsal fins set far back. Often curled on the bottom or wedged into crevices.

Mullus surmuletus.fieldNote: Pink-red body with two long chin barbels swept under the snout; flicks the barbels through sand to feed.

Trisopterus minutus.fieldNote: Small bib-shaped cod relative with a single chin barbel and big eyes; loose shoals in clear water.

Gadus morhua.fieldNote: Heavy-bodied cod with three dorsal and two anal fins, a chin barbel, and a curved pale lateral line.

Chelon labrosus.fieldNote: Silver streamlined mullet with very thick upper lip; cruises near the surface in tight schools.

Dicentrarchus labrax.fieldNote: Sleek silver bass with sharp spiny first dorsal; powerful and fast, often in small packs hunting baitfish.

Trachurus trachurus.fieldNote: Iridescent silver body with a strongly arched lateral line of bony scutes; tight dense schools.

Scomber scombrus.fieldNote: Steel-blue back with wavy black vertical bars; pencil-shaped body and a row of finlets before the tail.

Sprattus sprattus.fieldNote: Tiny silver baitfish in vast shimmering bait balls; sharp belly keel.

Atherina presbyter.fieldNote: Slender semi-transparent fish with a silver lateral stripe; loose shoals near the surface.

Taurulus bubalis.fieldNote: Heavily armoured ambush predator with long head spines and a wide gape; matches the rock perfectly.

Lipophrys pholis.fieldNote: Tubby blenny with a continuous spiny dorsal and a long anal fin; no head tentacles (unlike tompot blenny). Pops in and out of crevices and rockpool gullies.

Spinachia spinachia.fieldNote: Pencil-thin fish with a row of tiny spines along the back and a slender tail wrist.
